question_text: According to the World Health Organization (WHO), health is defined as a state of complete physical, mental and social well-being, and not merely the absence of which of the following?

options.text: Absence of physical fitness and muscular strength
Absence of disease or infirmity
Absence of wealth and material possessions
Absence of academic qualifications and professional success

explanation: The WHO definition of health (1948) states: 'Health is a state of complete physical, mental and social well-being and not merely the absence of disease or infirmity.' This definition emphasises the holistic, positive nature of health beyond just being free from disease.
elimination_hints: The WHO definition specifically uses the phrase 'disease or infirmity.' A, C and D introduce concepts not in the WHO definition.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following correctly defines 'Health Education' and distinguishes it from merely providing medical treatment to individuals who are already sick?

options.text: Health Education is the same as medical treatment and involves prescribing medicines to patients in hospitals

Health Education is a process of providing learning experiences that enable individuals to make informed decisions about health practices, adopt healthy behaviours, and prevent disease through knowledge, attitude change and skill development
Health Education means forcing people to follow strict diet plans without their consent or understanding
Health Education is limited to distributing free medicines in rural areas without any awareness component

explanation: Health Education is a planned process of providing learning experiences that help individuals and communities gain knowledge, develop positive attitudes, and acquire skills to make informed health decisions and adopt healthy behaviours. Unlike medical treatment (which is curative), Health Education is primarily preventive and promotional in nature.
elimination_hints: A confuses education with treatment; C involves coercion (not education); D focuses only on medicine distribution without awareness. B correctly captures the educational, preventive and empowering nature.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following is a key principle of Health Education that emphasises that health education programmes should be designed according to the specific needs, cultural context and existing knowledge level of the target community?

options.text: The principle of universality, which states that one single health programme works equally well for every community in the world
The principle of community relevance, which states that health education must be tailored to the specific needs, cultural beliefs, language and existing health knowledge of the target population
The principle of exclusion, which states that health education should only target educated urban populations
The principle of secrecy, which states that health information should be kept confidential and not shared with the general public

explanation: A core principle of Health Education is community relevance — programmes must be tailored to the target population's specific health needs, cultural context, beliefs, language, literacy level and existing knowledge. A one-size-fits-all approach is ineffective because health behaviours are deeply influenced by cultural, social and economic factors.
elimination_hints: A promotes a uniform approach (ineffective); C excludes populations who need it most; D contradicts the sharing/awareness purpose of Health Education.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Analyze why the WHO definition of health, which includes mental and social well-being alongside physical well-being, is considered more comprehensive than earlier definitions that focused only on the absence of physical disease.

options.text: The WHO definition is not actually more comprehensive; it simply uses more words to say the same thing as earlier definitions

The WHO definition recognises that true health is multi-dimensional — a person who is physically disease-free but suffering from severe depression, social isolation or chronic stress cannot be considered healthy, making the inclusion of mental and social dimensions essential for a complete understanding of health
The WHO definition focuses only on mental health and ignores physical health completely
The WHO definition is outdated and has been replaced by definitions that focus exclusively on genetic factors

explanation: The WHO definition is comprehensive because it recognises health as multi-dimensional: physical, mental and social. A person may be disease-free physically but suffer from depression (mental), loneliness (social) or chronic stress — and cannot be considered truly healthy. This holistic view acknowledges the interconnectedness of all health dimensions.
elimination_hints: A denies the additional dimensions; C wrongly claims physical health is ignored; D is factually incorrect (WHO definition remains current).
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Communicable diseases are those diseases that can spread from one person to another through various modes of transmission. Which of the following is an example of a communicable disease?

options.text: Diabetes mellitus (type 2), which is caused by insulin resistance and lifestyle factors
Tuberculosis (TB), which is caused by the bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis and spreads through airborne droplets
Osteoarthritis, which is caused by wear and tear of joint cartilage over time
Hypertension (high blood pressure), which is caused by genetic and lifestyle factors

explanation: Tuberculosis (TB) is a communicable (infectious) disease caused by the bacterium Mycobacterium tuberculosis. It spreads from person to person through airborne droplets when an infected person coughs, sneezes or speaks. Diabetes, osteoarthritis and hypertension are non-communicable diseases.
elimination_hints: Diabetes (A), osteoarthritis (C) and hypertension (D) are all non-communicable (cannot spread person-to-person). Only TB (B) is caused by an infectious agent and spreads between people.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following correctly describes the meaning of 'epidemic' as used in public health terminology, and provides an appropriate example of an epidemic situation?

options.text: An epidemic is a disease that affects only one individual and never spreads to anyone else in the community

An epidemic is the rapid spread of a disease affecting an unusually large number of people in a specific area or population within a short period, such as the outbreak of cholera in a district after flooding contaminated the water supply
An epidemic refers to any disease that has existed in a population for centuries without any change in its occurrence rate
An epidemic is a term used exclusively for diseases that affect only animals and never humans

explanation: An epidemic is the occurrence of a disease in a community or region at a rate clearly exceeding normal expectations, affecting an unusually large number of people within a short time period. A cholera outbreak after flooding is a classic example — contaminated water causes rapid spread among many people in the affected area.
elimination_hints: A describes a single case (not an epidemic); C describes an endemic disease (stable long-term presence); D incorrectly limits epidemics to animals (that would be an epizootic).
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Analyze the role of both formal and non-formal channels in delivering Health Education to the general population, and identify which option correctly lists examples of each channel type.

options.text: Formal channels include social media and WhatsApp groups; non-formal channels include school curricula and university courses

Formal channels include school health education curricula, college courses and structured government health programmes; non-formal channels include community workshops, health camps, mass media campaigns, street plays and peer education
Both formal and non-formal channels are identical and include only hospital-based patient counselling
Formal channels include only religious institutions; non-formal channels include only pharmaceutical companies

explanation: Formal channels are structured, institutionalised delivery systems — school curricula, college courses, government health programmes with defined objectives and assessments. Non-formal channels are flexible, community-based approaches — workshops, health camps, mass media (TV, radio, social media), street plays (nukkad natak), posters and peer education programmes.
elimination_hints: A reverses formal and non-formal; C limits to hospitals only; D assigns incorrect categories. B correctly identifies structured institutional delivery (formal) vs community-based flexible delivery (non-formal).
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Evaluate the importance of Health Education in the context of India's public health challenges, particularly regarding the dual burden of communicable and non-communicable diseases faced by the country.

options.text: Health Education is irrelevant in India because the country has already eliminated all communicable diseases and non-communicable diseases are not a concern

Health Education is critically important because India faces a dual burden — communicable diseases like TB, malaria and dengue persist in many regions, while non-communicable diseases like diabetes, heart disease and cancer are rapidly increasing due to urbanisation and lifestyle changes, requiring both preventive awareness and behaviour modification at the population level
Health Education is needed only in urban India because rural areas have no health problems
Health Education should focus exclusively on treatment and ignore prevention entirely

explanation: India faces a unique dual burden: communicable diseases (TB, malaria, dengue, typhoid) remain significant, especially in rural and underserved areas, while non-communicable diseases (diabetes, cardiovascular disease, cancer, obesity) are rising rapidly with urbanisation, sedentary lifestyles and dietary changes. Health Education addressing both through prevention, awareness and behaviour change is critically important.
elimination_hints: A makes a false claim about disease elimination; C ignores rural health challenges; D contradicts Health Education's preventive focus. B accurately captures the dual burden.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Which of the following modes of transmission is responsible for the spread of waterborne communicable diseases such as cholera, typhoid and hepatitis A in communities with poor sanitation?

options.text: Airborne transmission through droplets when an infected person coughs or sneezes
Contaminated water and food transmission (faecal-oral route) due to poor sanitation and unsafe drinking water
Transmission through direct skin-to-skin contact during handshakes
Transmission through insect bites such as mosquito bites

explanation: Waterborne diseases like cholera, typhoid and hepatitis A spread through the faecal-oral route — when water or food is contaminated with the faeces of an infected person due to poor sanitation, inadequate sewage systems or unsafe drinking water sources. Consuming contaminated water or food causes infection.
elimination_hints: A describes airborne diseases (TB, influenza); C describes contact transmission; D describes vector-borne diseases (malaria, dengue). Waterborne diseases spread through contaminated water/food (B).
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Evaluate which of the following objectives of Health Education is most fundamental, as it serves as the foundation upon which all other objectives — attitude change, skill development and behaviour modification — are built.

options.text: Generating revenue for pharmaceutical companies through increased medication sales

Providing accurate and evidence-based health knowledge and information to individuals, because informed understanding is the essential prerequisite for developing positive health attitudes, acquiring self-care skills and making voluntary behaviour changes
Enforcing strict government penalties for unhealthy behaviours without providing any information or education
Replacing all traditional medical systems with only modern Western medicine

explanation: The most fundamental objective of Health Education is providing accurate health knowledge and information. Knowledge is the foundation — individuals must first understand health facts before they can develop positive attitudes, acquire practical self-care skills, and voluntarily modify unhealthy behaviours. Without knowledge, lasting behaviour change cannot occur.
elimination_hints: A is a commercial motive, not an educational objective; C uses punishment instead of education; D is about medical systems, not health education. B correctly identifies knowledge as the foundational objective.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Match the following communicable diseases in Column A with their primary mode of transmission in Column B.

column_b.text: Airborne droplet transmission when infected person coughs or sneezes
Vector-borne transmission through the bite of infected female Anopheles mosquito
Direct contact transmission through skin-to-skin contact or shared contaminated objects
Waterborne transmission through contaminated water and food (faecal-oral route)

explanation: Malaria → Anopheles mosquito bite, vector-borne (Q). TB → airborne droplets from coughing (P). Cholera → contaminated water, faecal-oral (S). Ringworm → direct skin contact or shared objects (R).
elimination_hints: Malaria (1) is the classic mosquito-borne disease (Q); cholera (3) is the classic waterborne disease (S). Anchor with these two well-known associations.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Match the following dimensions of health in Column A with the specific indicator or example that best represents each dimension in Column B.

column_b.text: Ability to form positive relationships, cooperate with others and contribute meaningfully to community life
Freedom from disease, adequate organ function, physical fitness, and ability to perform daily activities without undue fatigue
Ability to manage and express feelings appropriately, cope with stress, and maintain emotional stability during challenges
Ability to think clearly, learn effectively, make rational decisions and maintain psychological well-being

explanation: Physical health → disease-free, organ function, fitness (Q). Mental health → clear thinking, learning, psychological well-being (S). Social health → relationships, cooperation, community contribution (P). Emotional health → managing feelings, coping with stress, emotional stability (R).
elimination_hints: Physical health (1) clearly relates to 'freedom from disease' and 'physical fitness' (Q); social health (3) clearly relates to 'relationships' and 'community' (P).
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Match the following objectives of Health Education in Column A with the specific outcome or action they aim to achieve in Column B.

column_b.text: Individuals develop a positive disposition towards healthy practices and reject harmful habits
Individuals adopt and sustain healthy behaviours in their daily lives over the long term
Individuals acquire practical abilities such as preparing balanced meals, performing CPR or practising proper hand hygiene
Individuals gain accurate information and understanding about health topics, diseases, nutrition and hygiene

explanation: Knowledge objective → gaining accurate health information (S). Attitude objective → developing positive health disposition (P). Skill objective → acquiring practical abilities like CPR, hand hygiene (R). Behaviour objective → adopting and sustaining healthy behaviours long-term (Q).
elimination_hints: Knowledge (1) clearly matches 'information and understanding' (S); skill (3) clearly matches 'practical abilities like CPR' (R).
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding the importance of Health Education in preventing communicable diseases.
assertion: Health Education plays a vital role in preventing the spread of communicable diseases by teaching people about hygiene practices, vaccination, safe water and sanitation.
reason: When individuals understand how diseases spread and adopt preventive behaviours such as handwashing, using clean water, maintaining sanitation and getting vaccinated, the chain of infection is broken and disease transmission is reduced.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — Health Education is vital for preventing communicable diseases. The Reason is also true and correctly explains the mechanism: education leads to understanding of disease transmission → adoption of preventive behaviours → broken chain of infection → reduced transmission.
elimination_hints: Both statements are well-established public health principles. The Reason explains precisely how Health Education achieves disease prevention — through knowledge-driven behaviour change that breaks the transmission chain.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding the WHO definition of health and its implications.
assertion: The WHO definition of health has been criticised by some scholars for being idealistic and practically unattainable, as very few individuals can claim to be in a state of 'complete' physical, mental and social well-being simultaneously.
reason: The WHO definition was formulated in 1948 and has never been revised or updated since its adoption.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — the WHO definition has been criticised for being idealistic because 'complete' well-being in all three dimensions is practically unattainable for most people. The Reason is also true — the core WHO definition in the Constitution (1948) has not been formally amended. However, the Reason does not explain the Assertion; the criticism of idealism is about the word 'complete,' not about when the definition was written.
elimination_hints: Both statements are factually correct, but the Reason (date of formulation) does not explain why the definition is considered idealistic (the word 'complete' does). So both are true but the Reason is not the explanation.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Read the Assertion and Reason below and select the correct option regarding communicable diseases and their prevention.
assertion: Vaccination is one of the most effective methods of preventing communicable diseases such as polio, measles, hepatitis B and tetanus.
reason: Vaccines stimulate the immune system to produce antibodies against specific pathogens, creating immunity without causing the actual disease, so that the body can fight the pathogen quickly if exposed in the future.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true, and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion
Both Assertion and Reason are true, but Reason is NOT the correct explanation of Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

explanation: The Assertion is true — vaccination is highly effective against many communicable diseases. The Reason is also true and correctly explains how vaccines work: they stimulate antibody production without causing disease, creating acquired immunity that enables rapid response upon future exposure.
elimination_hints: Both statements are established immunology facts. The Reason directly explains the biological mechanism (antibody production → immunity) that makes vaccination effective.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Arrange the following steps in the chain of infection for a communicable disease in the correct order, starting from the source and ending with the new host becoming infected.
items: I. The pathogen enters the new susceptible host through a portal of entry (mouth, nose, skin wound)
II. The infectious agent (pathogen) exists in a reservoir or source of infection (infected person, animal, soil)
III. The pathogen exits the reservoir through a portal of exit (coughing, sneezing, faeces, blood)
IV. The pathogen is transmitted to a new host through a mode of transmission (direct contact, airborne, waterborne, vector)

options.text: II → III → IV → I
I → IV → III → II
III → II → I → IV
IV → I → II → III

explanation: Chain of infection sequence: Pathogen in reservoir (II) → exits through portal of exit (III, e.g., coughing) → transmitted via mode of transmission (IV, e.g., airborne droplets) → enters new host through portal of entry (I, e.g., inhalation). Breaking any link in this chain prevents disease spread.
elimination_hints: The chain begins with the pathogen in its source/reservoir (II) and ends with the new host becoming infected (I). Anchor with these endpoints.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Arrange the following components of the three dimensions of health as stated in the WHO definition in the correct order as they appear in the original WHO definition statement.

options.text: II → III → I
I → II → III
III → I → II
I → III → II

explanation: The WHO definition states: 'Health is a state of complete physical, mental and social well-being...' The order as it appears in the original definition is: physical (II) → mental (III) → social (I).
elimination_hints: The WHO definition always lists them as 'physical, mental and social' — this is the standard order taught in all Health Education textbooks.
subject: Physical Education

question_text: True or False: Communicable diseases are caused by pathogenic microorganisms such as bacteria, viruses, fungi or parasites, and can be spread directly or indirectly from one person to another.

options.text: True
False

explanation: This statement is True. Communicable (infectious) diseases are caused by pathogenic microorganisms — bacteria (TB, cholera), viruses (influenza, COVID-19), fungi (ringworm) or parasites (malaria). They spread directly (person-to-person contact) or indirectly (through water, food, air, vectors).
elimination_hints: This is a standard definition of communicable diseases covering all four major pathogen categories (bacteria, viruses, fungi, parasites) and both transmission modes (direct, indirect).
subject: Physical Education

question_text: Study the diagram below that classifies common diseases into communicable and non-communicable categories along with their causative agents and modes of spread. Based on the diagram, which of the following diseases is INCORRECTLY classified if it appears under the 'non-communicable' category?

options.text: Diabetes mellitus — correctly placed under non-communicable because it is caused by metabolic dysfunction and cannot spread between people
Hypertension — correctly placed under non-communicable because it is caused by genetic and lifestyle factors
Dengue fever — INCORRECTLY placed under non-communicable because it is actually a communicable disease transmitted by the Aedes mosquito carrying the dengue virus
Osteoarthritis — correctly placed under non-communicable because it results from age-related joint degeneration

explanation: Dengue fever is a communicable disease caused by the dengue virus transmitted through the bite of infected Aedes aegypti mosquitoes. If placed under 'non-communicable' in the diagram, it is incorrectly classified. Diabetes, hypertension and osteoarthritis are all correctly non-communicable.
elimination_hints: Dengue is transmitted by mosquitoes (vector-borne) — it is clearly communicable. Diabetes, hypertension and osteoarthritis cannot be 'caught' from another person.
subject: Physical Education
